creator: void@f-m.fm
text:
Hi,

I've tried to get electron22 from pkg.freebsd.org (latest) but it's not there.

for a test, I accessed a dual-stack machine and tried compiling it from a freshly fetched ports tree. The machine has 19GB 'free' in top and 171GB free space on the disk.

/etc/make.conf has the following:

###
WITH_CCACHE_BUILD=YES
CCACHE_DIR=/var/cache/ccache

WITH_MANCOMPRESS=YES
OPTIONS_UNSET+=BRACKETEDPASTE FLANG
DEFAULT_VERSIONS+=ImageMagick=7
LICENSES_ACCEPTED+=GPLv2+ MSPAT PDFlib

SCCACHE_DIR=    /root/.sccache
OVERLAYS+=      /usr/local/share/sccache/overlay

###
/usr/ports/devel/electron22# make -DBATCH install

To build electron, you should have around 2GB of memory
and around 14 GB of free disk space.

===>  License BSD3CLAUSE LGPL21 MPL11 MIT accepted by the user
===>   electron22-22.3.1_1 depends on file: /usr/local/sbin/pkg - found
===>   electron22-22.3.1_1 depends on package: yarn-node16>0 - found
if [ ! -f /usr/ports/distfiles/electron/electron-yarn-cache-22.3.1.tar.xz ];  then /bin/mkdir -p /usr/ports/devel/electron22/work;  echo 'yarn-offline-mirror "./yarn-offline-cache"' >>  /usr/ports/devel/electron22/work/.yarnrc;  cp -f /usr/ports/devel/electron22/files/package.json /usr/ports/devel/electron22/files/yarn.lock /usr/ports/devel/electron22/work;  cd /usr/ports/devel/electron22/work && /usr/bin/env HOME=/usr/ports/devel/electron22/work XDG_CACHE_HOME=/usr/ports/devel/electron22/work/.cache  yarn --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts;  /usr/sbin/mtree -cbnSp yarn-offline-cache | /usr/sbin/mtree -C | /usr/bin/sed  -e 's:time=[0-9.]*:time=1677373220.000000000:'  -e 's:\([gu]id\)=[0-9]*:\1=0:g'  -e 's:flags=.*:flags=none:'  -e 's:^\.:./yarn-offline-cache:' > yarn-offline-cache.mtree;  /usr/bin/tar cJf /usr/ports/distfiles/electron/electron-yarn-cache-22.3.1.tar.xz  @yarn-offline-cache.mtree;  /bin/rm -f -r /usr/ports/devel/electron22/work;  fi
